name: Lincare - Buffalo (Orchard Park)
summary: Erie County location of a national respiratory-care and durable medical equipment supplier providing oxygen therapy, sleep apnea equipment and in-home set-up and instruction.
source_notes: First-party location page ('Buffalo Lincare'): address 40 Centre Drive, Suite 700, Orchard Park, NY 14127 (Erie County - corrects the lead's vaguer 'Jamestown/Orchard Park/Canisteo' listing). Exact wording: '24/7 support and in-home instruction and set-up', 'respiratory and oxygen care', 'durable medical equipment', goal to 'keep you safe at home and out of the hospital.' Primarily an in-home service run from an office address; the page does not state whether customers can walk in, so treat showroom visitability as unconfirmed. disability_literate false = not yet attested, not a negative finding. No premises-access statements - zero attribute claims.

name: Numotion - Buffalo (West Seneca)
summary: Appointment-only Erie County branch of a national complex rehab technology supplier providing power wheelchairs, custom seating systems and mobility equipment.
source_notes: First-party location page confirms '2440 North America Drive Suite 40 West Seneca, NY 14224'. The page states the branch operates 'By Appointment Only' and that 'Service' is 'Not offered onsite' - no public retail floor, so listed as a provider rather than a place; customers visit only by scheduled appointment. Classified provider on that first-party basis. disability_literate false = not yet attested (CRT is disability-focused equipment, but no competence self-attestation captured). No statements about the premises' own physical access - zero attribute claims.

name: People First Mobility
summary: Buffalo-based complex rehab and durable medical equipment provider offering in-home evaluations, equipment setup, training, repair and free loaner equipment for people with long-term disabilities, with assessments also available at its Hertel Avenue office.
source_notes: First-party site: address 800 Hertel Avenue, Suite 103, Buffalo, NY 14207 confirmed on both homepage and services page. Exact wording (services page): 'We offer in-home evaluations, free delivery, equipment setup and training, equipment repair, certified Assisted Technology Professionals (ATPs), certified repair technicians, free loaner equipment and hands-on trials'; 'We serve those with long-term developmental disabilities, cerebral palsy, mutliple sclerosis, muscular dystrophy, traumatic brain injury, Alzheimer's and dementia... the area's leading provider of pediatric mobility equipment.' Assessments happen 'at your home, school, Physical/Occupational therapist office, doctors office or our office' - the office is visitable by arrangement but no retail showroom is described. disability_literate true is grounded in the first-party certified-ATP statement and disability-specific service focus; still subject to formal self-attestation. The lead's 'division of People Inc.' claim was NOT found on the pages fetched (the /about-us page returned 403) - hence org_status_unconfirmed; verify affiliation before displaying it. No premises-access statements - zero attribute claims.

name: Quick Access LLC
summary: Locally owned, family-run Buffalo-area business that installs and services stairlifts, modular and portable ramps, automatic door openers and grab bars in customers' homes; no public showroom.
source_notes: First-party site (live): exact product wording 'Stairlifts - Ramps - Door Openers - Used stairlifts - Modular Ramps - Portable Ramps - Service & Grab bars'; self-describes as 'a local family-owned business'; phone 716-359-9419 (Buffalo area code); Buffalo-local presence corroborated by Buffalo-based HomeAdvisor/Angi listings. The lead's street address (331 University Ave, Buffalo 14223) does NOT appear on the site and was not confirmed anywhere first-party - omitted rather than repeated. In-home installation service with no showroom found; can be listed honestly only as a service provider. No premises-access statements - zero attribute claims. disability_literate false = not yet attested.

name: Ace Employment Services (Restoration Society, Inc.)
summary: A vocational rehabilitation program of Restoration Society, Inc. helping people with primary mental health conditions choose, get, and maintain competitive employment, including Ticket to Work services for SSI/SSDI beneficiaries.
source_notes: First-party program page (fetched OK). Exact wording: offers 'a diverse range of vocational rehabilitation services that assist customers to choose, get and maintain preferred competitive employment positions'; participants 'have a primary mental health condition and desire to gain competitive employment status.' Buffalo site 327 Elm Street confirmed (second site in Batavia, Genesee County). Parent Restoration Society is not among seeded orgs. No physical-access statements found.

name: Aspire of WNY - Environmental Modifications (EMODS) & Assistive Technology
summary: Nonprofit program that helps people with developmental disabilities obtain funding for and coordinate installation of home modifications (ramps, lifts, accessible bathrooms) and adaptive technologies; the work is delivered in the client's home, not at a showroom.
source_notes: First-party service page (live 2026-07): 'The Environmental Modification & Assistive Technology (EMODS) team provides assistance to obtain funding for environmental modifications and adaptive technologies...'; referrals via 716.838.0047 option 1 / intake@aspirewny.org. Two lead-list rows merged into this one record - the site shows a single combined EMODS & AT program; no separate orthotics/AT-training service page found. Address corrected: the lead's 4635 Union Rd Cheektowaga is Aspire's Center for Learning (an educational program); the org's contact page lists only 2356 N Forest Rd, Getzville NY 14068 (Erie County). In-home coordination service, no public showroom. Caveat: a stale search snippet suggested a 4/17/24 referral pause, but the live page shows active referral instructions - re-check status at import. disability_literate reflects that this is an OPWDD disability-services nonprofit per its own site; formal self-attestation still needed. No statements about their own premises' physical access - zero attribute claims.

name: Beyond Support Network — Employment Services (Tri-Main Center)
summary: The Buffalo employment-services site of Beyond Support Network offering Community Pre-Vocational Services and SEMP supported employment for people with developmental disabilities.

Yes' flag, one conservative entrance claim per rules.

name: The Buffalo Therapeutic Riding Center, Inc.
summary: Accredited therapeutic horseback riding program for children with emotional, developmental, or physical challenges ages 5-18, housed in one of the largest indoor riding arenas in the nation.
source_notes: Org website blocks automated fetch (HTTP 403); details from search excerpts of thebtrc.org pages (/facility, /contact, /directions). Stated mission: 'to enhance the development and quality of life for special needs children in Western New York by providing an accredited therapeutic riding program in a safe and nurturing environment.' Address 950 Amherst St, Buffalo NY 14216 confirmed across thebtrc.org excerpts, GuideStar, and VolunteerWNY. Facility is shared with Buffalo Equestrian Center / SBS Farms (same building). Re-verify by manual visit to the site. No explicit physical-access statements found; therapeutic riding itself has no schema key.

name: Center for Self-Advocacy Inc.
summary: A Buffalo self-advocacy organization that works to advocate for equality and empower individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities to actively participate in society.
source_notes: First-party site (fetched OK). Purpose wording: 'advocate for equality and empower individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities to actively participate in society'; homepage: 'We help people with developmental disabilities work, advocate within their communities, and have an independent and productive lifestyle.' About page describes leadership and peer-mentor staff with lived disability experience (self-advocate-led org) — basis for disabled_led, but NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING. Shares 1021 Broadway with the already-seeded SANYS Western Region but is a distinct incorporated org. No physical-access statements found.

name: Community Services for Every1
summary: A Buffalo human-services agency specializing in support for people with intellectual/developmental disabilities, people with chronic health and complex mental health concerns, and survivors of domestic violence, with adult services including vocational supports.
source_notes: WEBSITE CORRECTED: lead's csevery1.org does not resolve (DNS dead); live first-party site is csevery1.com (fetched OK). Site wording: specializes in support for 'individuals with developmental disabilities, youth and adults with chronic health conditions and complex mental health concerns, and survivors of domestic violence'; banner 'Empowering Lives Through Connection.' HQ 180 Oak St. confirmed. Employment/pre-voc services per the lead and OPWDD/ACCES-VR referral model, but the fetched homepage shows 'Adult Services' generally — confirm the employment program wording at onboarding. No physical-access statements found.

name: Danceability, Inc.
summary: Individualized dance, fitness and movement program (tap, jazz, ballet, fitness) for children and adults with disabilities, running September through May.
source_notes: Org website (fetched 2026-07-10). Exact self-description: 'Danceability is an individualized dance, fitness and movement program serving the special needs community.' Site notes it is in its 19th season, confirming current operation. Address confirmed on site. No physical-access statements on site; no disability-led wording. This lead appeared twice in the input (Adaptive Sports + Arts Inclusive Programs) — merged into one record.

name: Developmental Disabilities Alliance of Western New York (DDAWNY)
summary: A collaborative alliance of member voluntary agencies across Western New York that provide services to people with developmental disabilities and advocate on developmental-disability issues.
source_notes: First-party site (fetched OK). Exact wording: 'The Developmental Disabilities Alliance of Western New York is a collaborative group of member voluntary agencies that provide services to people with developmental disabilities.' ADDRESS CORRECTED: lead gave 1219 North Forest Rd., Williamsville (People Inc. HQ — stale); the site lists 960 W. Maple Ct., Elma, NY 14059 (Erie County). Membership coalition, not a direct-service provider — disability_literate true on advocacy mission. No physical-access statements found.

name: Excalibur Leisure Skills Center, Inc.
street: 1111 Fuhrmann Blvd (Safe Harbor Marina, Buffalo Harbor State Park)
summary: All-volunteer nonprofit providing adaptive boating and fishing excursions on a US Coast Guard-certified wheelchair-accessible vessel moored at Buffalo Harbor State Park.
source_notes: Org website (fetched 2026-07-10). Self-description: 'Dedicated to providing adaptive boating and fishing opportunities for developmentally challenged and disadvantaged persons.' Site states the vessel 'Richard A. Smith' 'is moored at the Wheelchair Access Dock. Address: 1111 Fuhrmann Blvd. Buffalo, New York, 14203' and 'It is wheelchair accessible, and on average, we can accommodate 6 wheelchairs.' These are vessel/dock claims with no schema key (not entrance/restroom/parking), so zero attributes recorded. Office/mailing address is 90 North Drive, Buffalo NY 14216. Status caveat: current weekday schedule is posted but the newest first-party news item is from 2020 — confirm 2026 season by phone before import.

name: FitnessAbility, Inc.
summary: Not-for-profit universally integrated fitness facility offering inclusive fitness and recreation programming, including Special Olympics programs.
source_notes: Org website (fetched 2026-07-10, site dated 2026). Describes itself as a 'universally integrated fitness facility' and a not-for-profit with the mission 'We will serve our participants with the Highest Quality client experience in a safe, clean, friendly, and fun environment. No man, woman, or child left behind.' Lists Special Olympics programming. disability_literate=true rests on the universally-integrated/special-needs programming focus; the mission statement itself is general. No physical-access statements; no disability-led wording.

name: Goodwill of Western New York
summary: A nonprofit provider of education, training, and career services for people with barriers to employment, including the Goodskills Career Builder program, headquartered on William Street in Buffalo.
source_notes: First-party site (fetched OK); active, current 2025 news. Mission wording: 'Goodwill empowers people to achieve their fullest potential through the power of work'; 'a nonprofit provider of education, training, and career services for people with barriers to employment' that assists 'people with all types of vocational challenges and barriers to employment.' NOT in either seed batch (checked — no Goodwill overlap). disability_literate left FALSE conservatively: fetched pages say 'barriers to employment,' never disability-specific mission wording — confirm at onboarding (historically a vocational-rehabilitation provider). No physical-access statements found.

name: High Hurdles Therapeutic Riding Program (SASi)
summary: Therapeutic horseback riding program of SASi for individuals age 3+ with intellectual and developmental disabilities, with a 2026 season that began March 2026.
source_notes: First-party site exists (note: no 'www' — www.highhurdlestrc.org does not resolve) but returned empty content to automated fetch (JS-rendered); flag used to mark fetch-reverify need. Details corroborated via search excerpts of highhurdlestrc.org, sasinc.org (which 301-redirects its High Hurdles page to highhurdlestrc.org, confirming SASi affiliation), and 211wny.org provider 4512. Search-excerpt wording: 'High Hurdles seeks to provide a unique opportunity for people with special needs through the horse-human bond'; address 13339 Rt 39, Sardinia NY 14134; 2026 registration opened with first session March 2, 2026 (operating). Sardinia is in southern Erie County. No physical-access statements found.

name: Inclusive Theater of WNY (ITOWNY)
summary: Community theater company producing original scripts with casts chosen solely on ability, whether or not the actor has a disability.
source_notes: Org website (fetched 2026-07-10). Exact self-description: 'Inclusive Theater of WNY seeks to reimagine our society through producing original scripts in our ongoing writing group and writing workshops, and through the production and performance of those scripts with actors who are chosen solely on their ability—whether they have a disability or not.' Also: 'ITOWNY is committed to diversity at all levels: on our stage, in our audience, on our staff, and within our leadership.' Address per site: 'The Foundry, 298 Northampton Street, Buffalo, NY. 14208' (matches lead); some performances at St. Mary's HS, Lancaster. The lead's 'disability-led' claim is NOT stated first-party — disabled_led left false; NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING. Status caveat: newest listed performances ran through December 2025; confirm 2026 season activity. No physical-access statements. ORG-STATUS RE-VERIFY 2026-07-10: not defunct, but the newest first-party activity is 2025 (It's a Wonderful Life, Dec 5-14 2025; most recent post Nov 15 2025) — NO 2026 season is posted. Present as awaiting-2026-season; re-verify before surfacing as currently producing.

name: IndicatorArt
summary: 501(c)(3) art-education nonprofit offering painting workshops and gallery shows, online and in person, for people with developmental disabilities.
source_notes: Org website (fetched 2026-07-10). Exact self-description: 'Enriching the lives of people with developmental disabilities through the creative arts.' Registered 501(c)(3); actively operating with current classes and galleries. Based in Buffalo but no fixed public street address published (programs are online and in-person at varying locations) — street null per rules. No physical-access statements; no disability-led wording.

name: Innovative Placements
summary: A WNY-based job-placement service specializing in employment for people with disabilities as a partner of ACCES-VR and the New York State Commission for the Blind.
source_notes: First-party site (fetched OK). Exact wording: 'Innovative Placements specializes in job placement for people with disabilities and is a partner of Adult Career and Continuing Education Services-Vocational Rehabilitation (ACCES-VR) and the New York State Commission for the Blind (NYSCB)'; tagline 'Jobs for people with disabilities.' Only a PO Box (PO Box 801, Buffalo, NY 14201) is published — no public street address, services delivered in the community. 20+ years serving WNY per site. No physical-access statements found.

name: LiNK — Buffalo Jewish Federation (disability inclusion, now branded 'Belonging')
summary: The Buffalo Jewish Federation's disability-inclusion effort (LiNK Inclusion Planning Team, work now branded 'Belonging') providing outreach, advocacy, and program support so people with disabilities are fully included in Jewish community life.

Program-name transition (LiNK → Belonging) unconfirmed on a live fetch — reverify name at import. Address 338 Harris Hill Rd., Suite 108B is the Federation office per search results; program has no separate site. No physical-access statements found.

name: Lothlorien Therapeutic Riding Center
summary: Therapeutic riding center offering equine-assisted activities for individuals with special needs, veterans, and anxiety support groups, just outside the Village of East Aurora.
source_notes: Org website (fetched 2026-07-10, site dated through 2026). Homepage gives location only as 'just outside of the Village of East Aurora, New York'; street address 15 Reiter Rd, East Aurora NY 14052 corroborated by the site's own contact page URL in search results plus 211wny.org and Yelp. No physical-access statements.

name: Miracle League of Western New York
summary: Adaptive baseball league for individuals with adaptive needs, played on a custom-designed rubberized field at Veterans Park in Grand Island.
source_notes: Org website (fetched 2026-07-10). Self-description from homepage: provides 'individuals with adaptive needs the opportunity to play baseball on a custom-designed, rubberized field, removing barriers and fostering inclusivity, joy, and community'; eligibility: 'Anyone with an adaptive need that cannot be accommodated in a traditional league.' Operating (season starts the Sunday after Memorial Day). Field address from first-party contact page: 'Veterans Park, 1717 Bedell Rd, Grand Island, NY 14072' (a search snippet said 1715 — the first-party page's 1717 is used); mailing address PO Box 833, Grand Island NY 14072. The rubberized-field claim is an adaptive feature with no schema key — no attributes recorded. Note: the lead also listed wnychallengersports.com (Challenger Sports, Lockport) — that is a separate org in Niagara County and was NOT recorded.

name: OPWDD Western New York Developmental Disabilities Regional Office (Region 1, West Seneca)
summary: The NYS Office for People With Developmental Disabilities regional office and Front Door access point for developmental-disability services in Region 1, with on-site clinical services including nursing, occupational therapy, physical therapy, psychology, social work, and speech pathology.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory field 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' with no further detail — mapped to a single conservative entrance claim only (general_accessible_flag_only). Needs first-person confirmation.

name: ParaCliffHangers Adaptive Climbing Meetup at Central Rock Gym Buffalo
summary: Recurring adaptive rock climbing meetup (first and third Mondays monthly) run with the nonprofit ParaCliffHangers at the Central Rock Gym Buffalo climbing facility.

Recorded as the disability-specific program, not the gym itself — the host venue is a general commercial climbing gym whose mission is not disability-focused. Lead's original claims about sensory-friendly sessions were NOT found first-party. A third-party directory calls the gym wheelchair accessible, but no first-party physical-access statement exists, so zero attributes. disability_literate applies to the adaptive program, not the gym; the gym should self-attest at onboarding.

name: People Inc. - Environmental Modifications (EMOD) & Adaptive Technologies
summary: Nonprofit program providing project management for OPWDD-funded home modifications (ramps, lifts, barrier-free showers, widened doorways), adaptive technology devices and vehicle modifications, delivered in clients' homes across Western New York.

WNY address 1219 North Forest Rd, Williamsville NY 14221 (org HQ - the service itself is in-home; no showroom). The lead's dedicated program URL 404s; the org's main services URL structure changed, so re-link at import if a dedicated service page reappears. disability_literate reflects OPWDD disability-services nonprofit status per its own materials; formal self-attestation still needed. No premises-access statements - zero attribute claims.

summary: Physical and occupational therapy center on the Excelsior Orthopaedics campus in Amherst, with hand, foot and ankle, and spine specialty centers.

Services per excerpts: 'General Orthopaedic evaluation and treatment, Physical & Occupational Therapy', Hand/Foot and Ankle/Spine centers. Re-verify page content manually. Ortho practice, no disability-specific program found: disability_literate false. ADA MDE recruitment target.

name: Family Care Physical Therapy
summary: Outpatient physical therapy practice with clinics on Kensington Avenue in Buffalo and on Sheridan Drive in Williamsville.
source_notes: Provider website is live (indexed pages: /about-us, /our-services, /appointments) but presented an expired TLS certificate at fetch time (2026-07-10) — treated per the 403 rule: details from search excerpts; re-verify. Excerpts of familycarept.com/appointments list two locations: '1515 Kensington Ave, Buffalo, NY 14215' and '5225 Sheridan Dr #21, Williamsville, NY 14221' (lead phone 716-565-0818 matches the Williamsville listing). Lead's '777 Hopkins Rd' address appears nowhere — replaced with the corroborated Buffalo site. Lead's stroke-recovery claim not verifiable first-party: disability_literate false pending site re-fetch. No access/MDE statements found — ADA MDE recruitment target.

name: First Step Physical Therapy
summary: One-on-one physical therapy clinic in Cheektowaga specializing in neurological rehabilitation for spinal cord injury, stroke, Parkinson's disease, and traumatic brain injury.
source_notes: Provider website (fetched 2026-07-10) confirms lead verbatim: 'First Step Physical Therapy, 2564 Walden Ave., Suite 105, Cheektowaga, NY 14225', phone (716) 288-7427. disability_literate true on explicit neuro-rehab programs: dedicated condition pages for spinal cord injury, stroke, Parkinson's ('tremors, stiffness, slowness, and balance problems'), and TBI; model quoted: '1 on 1 Physical Therapy... True Individualized Care: Just You and Your Therapist, Every Session.' No physical-access or MDE statements published — priority ADA MDE recruitment target given its wheelchair-using patient population.

name: Glover Physical Therapy and Pain Rehabilitation

source_notes: Provider website (homepage fetched 2026-07-10; address corroborated by the site's own /aboutus.html excerpt): '3620 Harlem Rd - Suite 2 Cheektowaga NY 14215', phone (716) 446-9500 matching lead; city corrected Buffalo→Cheektowaga. Services quoted: 'chronic pain syndromes; pain related to orthopedic injuries; general medical and orthopedic post-surgical pain'; treats 'neuromuscular disabilities, balance and gait abnormalities' (condition-list mention, not a named program — disability_literate kept false conservatively). No access/MDE statements — ADA MDE recruitment target.

name: Northtown Buffalo Physical Therapy

source_notes: Provider website (fetched 2026-07-10): 'Georgetown Square, 5225 Sheridan Dr. Suite #21, Williamsville, NY 14221'; phone 716-631-2626 matches lead. Lead's address (1515 Kensington Ave) actually belongs to Family Care Physical Therapy — corrected; lead's domain northtownpt.com shows only 'We're under construction' (replaced with the practice's live domain). Services quoted: 'Individualized Treatment' with a 'hands on approach to identify the source of your problem'; 'Stretch Out Program' for 'Preventative Care'. Caveat: Family Care PT's Williamsville listing cites the same suite — confirm current occupancy during outreach. No access/MDE statements — ADA MDE recruitment target. disability_literate false.

name: Nurse Practitioner-Adult Health PC (Adult Health DNP)
summary: Adult-health nurse practitioner practice specializing in medical cannabis certification and management, seeing patients in person at its Seneca Street office weekdays 9-5 with telehealth follow-ups.
source_notes: NYS OGS SDVOB certified, control number 232083; state API record shows physical address 2234 Seneca St Buffalo 14210, owner 100% ownership, isServiceDisabled=true (cert basis: >=51% ownership + day-to-day control by service-disabled veteran(s)). Practice site cannabisdoctorbuffalo.com confirms in-person patients at this address, Mon-Fri 9am-5pm, and describes the practice as veteran owned and operated. No accessibility features stated on the site, so no attribute claims. NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

Services quoted: 'Vestibular and Balance Impairments', 'Neurological Disorders', 'Gait Instability and Difficulty Walking', 'Total Joint Replacements' (condition list on a manual/sports practice — disability_literate false conservatively). Founder/CEO Walter R. Szymanski is a Physical Therapist. Services quoted: 'Physical Therapy' for 'orthopedic injuries, low back, and neck pain', 'pre & post-surgical rehabilitation', 'Vestibular Therapy' and 'Neurological Therapy' — the named neurological/vestibular service lines are the basis for disability_literate true. No access/MDE statements — ADA MDE recruitment target (OT services make exam-table/scale attestation directly relevant).

name: Buffalo Arts Studio
summary: Nonprofit arts center in the Tri-Main Center offering exhibitions, affordable studio space, and community arts education, with a published facility accessibility page.

attributes.note: All public restrooms are handicap accessible.
Mobility accessible parking spots are available in the Visitor and Access parking lots on Halbert between Phelps and Jewett Ave.

source_notes: Org website + dedicated first-party accessibility page (fetched 2026-07-10; note www subdomain does not resolve — use apex domain). Mission: 'Buffalo Arts Studio believes that artists and curators can effect change through purposeful collaborations that balance community needs, artistic insight, and educational impact.' Two attributes are explicit on the accessibility page (quotes recorded per attribute). Entrance is NOT claimed step-free: page says 'Visitors can access our space via accessible entrances, elevators, and reserved parking' and 'A wheelchair lift is available directly to your left upon entering the TriMain building' — general/lift-dependent wording, so no entrance_step_free claim; general_accessible_flag_only covers it. Honest caveat also on page: 'Visitors will encounter industrial concrete floors that may have uneven surfaces and raised transitions in the lobby and hallways.' Correct address is 2495 Main St Suite 500 (the lead said 2495 Main St; Suite 443 next door is Arts for Learning WNY — different org). Not a disability-mission org; recorded as an arts venue. Operating; site notes a temporary closure June 28-July 6, 2026 (passed).

name: ES & ES Enterprises, LLC (49 Express Pit Stop)
summary: Full-service gas station and convenience store (49 Express Pit Stop) selling fuel, bottled water, gifts, souvenirs, and camping supplies at 409 Bloomingdale Rd.
source_notes: Federal SBA VetCert SDVOSB, active since 08/01/2024 (UEI P4NPNZZLWGB2, CAGE 4TXK8; cert basis: >=51% service-disabled-veteran ownership + control; also identifies as Native American owned). SBA capabilities narrative states 'Bottled Water, Gas Station, Gifts, Novelties, Souvenirs, Camping Survival Supplies'; Yelp confirms the 49 Express Pit Stop gas station operates at the same 409 Bloomingdale Rd address. CAVEAT (reason for storefront_status_unconfirmed): the company's own site esandesenterprises.com presents only B2B first-responder/tactical equipment distribution - confirm the certified entity operates the retail station before import. SBA lists Genesee County (some registries give Basom NY 14013); zip 14001 is Akron. NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING. STOREFRONT LINK CONFIRMED 2026-07-10 (owner policy: include once a real operating location is verified): the certified SDVOSB LLC's federal SAM.gov entity registration lists 'ES & ES Enterprises, LLC' at 409 Bloomingdale Rd (Basom/Akron) with NAICS 'Gasoline Stations with Convenience Stores' + 'Gift/Novelty/Souvenir Stores', owner Eric W Smith; Yelp confirms the 49 Express Pit Stop operates at that address. The certified entity therefore operates the visitable retail station (the tactical B2B site is a separate line under the same LLC). Ownership remains SELF-ATTESTED per §12 (needs onboarding attestation); the entity-to-storefront link rests on federal SAM registration, not a single first-party statement.

name: Escarpment Arms, Inc.

source_notes: Dual-certified: NYS OGS SDVOB control number 221378 AND federal SBA SDVOSB active since 01/28/2022 (UEI EDPVJKD8R338, CAGE 8H3C5, profile https://search.certifications.sba.gov/profile/EDPVJKD8R338/8H3C5). Both certs require >=51% service-disabled-veteran ownership + control. Business site escarpmentarms.com confirms storefront hours (Tue-Fri 10-6, Sat 10-4). No accessibility features stated on the site, so no attribute claims. NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: LA FFOCA (L A FFOCA LLC)
summary: NYS-licensed adult-use retail cannabis dispensary with a walk-in storefront and posted daily hours in Niagara Falls.
source_notes: NYS OGS SDVOB certified, control number 242227; state API shows owner at 100% ownership with isServiceDisabled=true (cert basis: >=51% ownership + control). Also holds one of NY's first service-connected disabled veteran (SCDV) OCM cannabis retail licenses (OCM-RETL-24-000261 per findweedny.com). Storefront operation confirmed via laffoca.com and Leafly (posted retail hours, in-store pickup). EDITORIAL NOTE: prior sweep reviewed and excluded Combat Vet Cannabis (Erie) for unstated reasons - apply the same editorial standard here before import. NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: National Seating & Mobility - Buffalo (West Seneca)
summary: Erie County branch of a national complex rehab technology supplier offering custom wheelchairs, seating, stairlifts, ramps, medical supplies and equipment service and repair, with posted business hours.
source_notes: First-party branch page: address '19 Ransier Dr Ste 5B, West Seneca, NY 14224', phone (716) 674-0783. Exact wording: 'Custom Accessible Wheelchairs, Stairlifts & Ramps'; services listed include power and manual wheelchairs, cushions, custom molds, accessibility solutions, medical supplies, and service & repair. Posted opening time (8:30 AM) indicates a visitable branch, though CRT branches typically run scheduled evaluations rather than retail browsing - kept as a place on the posted-hours basis. Important: 'Accessibility' on their page is a PRODUCT category (things they sell), not a statement about their own premises - no attribute claims inferred. No premises-access statements found.

name: Pro2 LLC - CPAP Center (Williamsville)
summary: Walk-in Williamsville location of a Western New York respiratory equipment supplier providing CPAP/BiPAP fitting, setup and supplies plus home oxygen therapy, open to visitors during posted business hours.
source_notes: First-party locations page confirms '5759 Main St, Suite 200, Williamsville NY 14221' with posted hours (Mon-Fri 8:30am-1pm, 2pm-5pm) and states all three WNY locations are open to visitors during business hours - visitable retail-style premises, hence a place. Homepage self-description: 'Expert fitting, setup, and ongoing supply management for sleep apnea therapy' and 'reliable oxygen therapy equipment and regular tank refills.' Two sibling locations exist (1120 Youngs Rd Suite 100, Williamsville; 3586 California Rd, Orchard Park - both also Erie County) if the directory wants them as separate places later. Lead address confirmed as-is. No statements about the premises' own physical access - zero attribute claims.

Posted retail hours (Mon-Fri 9am-5pm, Sat 9am-3pm) indicate a walk-in storefront, hence a place. No statements about the store's own physical access (entrance, parking, restroom) - zero attribute claims.
